summary: The auth-flow feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. All 13 expected behaviors are satisfied: /auth/signup page with validation, /auth/signin page with error handling, /auth/callback route for session exchange, signout functionality in header, protected route redirection via middleware, and SSR cookie-based session persistence. All 41 tests pass including 6 signin tests and 7 signup tests. TypeScript and ESLint pass with no errors.

issues.description: The header.tsx component was added in commit dbe7df5 but the current version has enhanced functionality with mobile navigation that was added in a later commit (app-layout-navigation feature). This is expected as the header was enhanced across features.
issues.suggestedFix: No fix needed - the header was intentionally enhanced by the subsequent app-layout-navigation feature. The auth-flow commit established the base signout functionality.

sharedStateObservations: The Supabase SSR pattern for session management is well-established in src/utils/supabase/middleware.ts, server.ts, and client.ts. The pattern uses createServerClient for middleware/server and createBrowserClient for browser-side operations.
Protected routes are defined by protectedPrefixes in middleware.ts: ['/dashboard', '/plan', '/playbooks/submit']. This pattern should be documented for future route additions.
The signin/signup server actions use redirectTo parameter to preserve the user's intended destination after authentication. This pattern is implemented in signin-form.tsx and actions.ts.
Email confirmation flow is handled gracefully in signup - if email confirmation is required (session not created), the user sees a success message to check their email.
Client-side validation uses useTransition for async state management during form submission.
The auth callback route uses 'next' query parameter for flexible redirect after email confirmation.

commandsRun.observation: All 41 tests pass (1 client test, 5 page tests, 4 not-found tests, 4 dashboard tests, 14 header tests, 6 signin-form tests, 7 signup-form tests)

11 files created/modified: actions.ts, callback/route.ts, signin/page.tsx, signin-form.tsx, signin-form.test.tsx, signup/page.tsx, signup-form.tsx, signup-form.test.tsx, header.tsx, (protected)/layout.tsx, (protected)/dashboard/page.tsx

interactiveChecks.observed: Complete with email+password form, client-side validation (required fields, email format regex, min 6 char password), and server action for signup with error handling
Complete with email+password form, client-side validation, error handling for invalid credentials, and redirectTo parameter support
Complete with code exchange for session establishment and redirect to dashboard or error page
Signout server action implemented in actions.ts and used in Header component via form submission
middleware.ts redirects unauthenticated users from /dashboard, /plan/*, /playbooks/submit to /auth/signin with redirectTo param
(protected)/layout.tsx enforces auth at layout level and redirects to /auth/signin if not authenticated
Supabase SSR cookies properly configured in middleware.ts with setAll/getAll cookie handling

codeReview.summary:
The auth-flow implementation is comprehensive and production-ready. All expected behaviors from the feature specification are implemented:

1. /auth/signup: Complete form with validation (required fields, email format, min 6 chars password), duplicate email handling, and email confirmation flow
2. /auth/signin: Complete form with validation, invalid credential error messages, and redirectTo support
3. /auth/callback: Session exchange with redirect to dashboard or error page
4. Signout: Server action integrated in Header component
5. Protected routes: Middleware redirects unauthenticated users from /dashboard, /plan/*, /playbooks/submit
6. After signin redirect: Preserves redirectTo parameter and redirects to /dashboard
7. Session persistence: SSR cookie management via Supabase middleware

Code quality is excellent with proper TypeScript types, React hooks usage, accessibility attributes (aria-label, aria-invalid, role attributes), and comprehensive test coverage.
